LanguageData.Fluffy.Pharmacist.Tip: Not all wounds are equal; set guidelines for which medicine to use when.
LanguageData.Fluffy.Pharmacist.Settings.Title: Pharmacist settings
LanguageData.Fluffy.Pharmacist.Population.Prisoner.De: Prisoners in your colony, regardless of faction.
LanguageData.Fluffy.Pharmacist.Population.Animal.Desc: Animals which have been tamed by your colony.
LanguageData.Fluffy.Pharmacist.Population.Guest.Desc: Anything that doesn't fit into another category, such as guests or wildlife, will make use of these rules.
LanguageData.Fluffy.Pharmacist.Severity.Minor.Tip: Bruises, light bleeding, and other superficial injuries.
LanguageData.Fluffy.Pharmacist.Severity.LifeThreathen: Life-threatening wounds, heart attacks, and near-fatal diseases.
LanguageData.Fluffy.Pharmacist.Severity.Operation.Tip: Any and all surgical operations, regardless of type.
LanguageData.Fluffy.Pharmacist.Severity.LongTerm.Tip: Non-urgent illnesses that need regular tending, such as blood rot and gut worms. Also includes chronic conditions like asthma.
LanguageData.Fluffy.Pharmacist.DiseaseMargin.Tip:
Security margin used when treating diseases. 

If immunity is lower than disease progress plus this margin (and severity is higher than the minimum threshold), the disease is treated as a life-threatening injury.

For example, if a disease has progressed 75%, immunity is at 80%, and the margin is 10%, the disease will be classed as life-threatening. If the threshold was 5%, the disease would be classed as a major injury instead.
LanguageData.Fluffy.Pharmacist.DiseaseThreshold.Tip: Minimum severity before a disease can be classed as potentially lethal. \n\nIf severity is higher than this threshold (and immunity is lower than the severity plus security margin), the disease is treated as a life-threathening injury.
LanguageData.Fluffy.Pharmacist.MinorWoundsThreshold.T: If the number of minor cuts is greater than this threshold, they are treated as a major injury regardless of bleed rate. \n\nUseful to prevent 'death by a thousand cuts', where a large number of small cuts can cause multiple infections - and ultimately, death.
LanguageData.Fluffy.Pharmacist.SearchRadius.Tip: Doctors will only search for medicine within this radius. If none can be found, they will use only whatever they have in their inventory.
